stimulus: When ecologist Daniel Mennill played different forest recordings to groups of young songbirds, groups that heard the songs of an active, thriving population of their own species were the most ______blank. They were twice as likely as other groups to begin practicing their own songs. This suggests that playing recordings of a healthy population may encourage young birds to develop their songs.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale: Choice D is the best answer because "responsive" means reacting readily and is supported by the detail that the birds in those groups were twice as likely to begin practicing their songs. Choice A is incorrect because "silent" would contradict the fact that the birds began practicing songs. Choice B is incorrect because nothing in the text indicates the birds were afraid. Choice C is incorrect because "scattered" describes physical distribution, not how the birds reacted to the recordings.
